title: Another account
description: According to Harun b. Ishaq-Mus'ab b. al-Migdam- Isra'il -Abu Islhag63-I Iarithah-`Ali: When we came to Medina we ate of its produce and became jaded with it and unwell from eating it. [12891 The Messenger of God was making enquiries about Badr, and when we learnt that the polytheists had come the Messenger of God went to Badr which is a well) and we got there before the polytheists. We found there two of them, a man of Quraysh and a mawla of 'Uqbah b. Abi Mu`ayt.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 07
